behavioral.question: Tell me about yourself.
Why do you want to become a Cloud Engineer?
Why are you interested in our company?
Tell me about a time you learned a new cloud technology quickly.
Describe a project you're most proud of.
How do you stay up to date with cloud technologies?
Describe your ideal work environment.
What are your greatest strengths?
What is one area you're currently improving?
Where do you see yourself in five years?
Tell me about a time you solved a difficult production problem.
Describe a situation where you had to make a difficult technical decision.
Tell me about a time you disagreed with a teammate. How did you handle it?
Describe a situation where you had to learn a completely new technology under a tight deadline.
Tell me about a cloud migration that didn't go as planned. What did you learn?
Describe a situation where you improved reliability or reduced downtime.
Tell me about a time you optimized cloud costs.
Describe a time when you worked under significant operational pressure.
Tell me about a mistake you made in production and how you handled it.
Describe a situation where automation significantly improved your workflow.
Imagine a production outage occurs at 2 AM while you're on call. Explain exactly how you would respond, communicate with stakeholders, and restore service.
Your manager asks you to migrate a business-critical application to the cloud in half the estimated time. How would you respond?
A teammate repeatedly bypasses Infrastructure as Code processes and makes manual production changes. How would you handle the situation?
Senior leadership wants to reduce cloud costs immediately, while engineering prioritizes performance. Explain how you would balance both goals.

You are leading a global cloud modernization initiative involving multiple teams, tight deadlines, and millions of users. Explain how you would coordinate communication, prioritize work, manage risks, and ensure a successful delivery.
